tool.name: KMZtoOrganizedGDBWaterfall
tool.summary: Converts KMZ/KML inputs to a clean organized Geodatabase (or N Geodatabases) using a step-by-step waterfall of choices: (1) container shape, (2) split-into-Feature-Datasets toggle, (3a) Feature Dataset source if FDs are on, and (3b) Feature Class naming strategy. Parses popup HTML into typed attribute fields, adds Folder1/2/3 columns for folder context, and projects to a chosen coordinate system. Scratch folder is auto-created next to the chosen output and kept after the run for re-use.
tool.usage: Pick KMZ files and walk down the numbered questions. The downstream questions (3a, 3b) re-populate their options based on earlier answers. NetworkLink Options (collapsed) follow external KMZ references from inside source KMZs -- off by default; turn on when a source is a wrapper around live data feeds.

param.name: input_kmz
param.dialogReference: One or more KMZ or KML files. Each becomes its own per-input scratch GDB internally; the scratch folder is created automatically next to the chosen output (see "Output Geodatabase" or "Output Folder" depending on your container choice in 1).

param.name: container
param.dialogReference:
Where the converted features end up. This is the first decision and it shapes everything downstream.

ONE MERGED GDB -- All input KMZs combined into a single Geodatabase that you pick under "Output Geodatabase". Best for cross-source analysis and when the dataset is meant to live together.

ONE GDB PER SOURCE KMZ -- Each input KMZ produces its own separate Geodatabase file inside the folder you pick under "Output Folder". Best when each KMZ is a separate deliverable, or when input KMZs have unrelated schemas you don't want merged. The subsequent questions (2, 3a, 3b) still apply -- they determine the FD/FC layout INSIDE each per-KMZ GDB. The only restriction is that the "Source KMZ filename" FD source (3a) is hidden since the GDB itself already represents the source.

This choice affects which output picker is enabled and which Feature Dataset source options (3a) are available.

param.name: output_gdb
param.displayname: Output Geodatabase (used when container is 'One merged GDB')
param.dialogReference:
Path to the file Geodatabase you want to create. Required when container (1) is "One merged GDB"; disabled otherwise.

If the GDB already exists, the tool refuses to overwrite -- delete it (or pick a new path) before running. A scratch folder is auto-created at <parent>\<stem>_scratch\ to hold intermediate per-KMZ GDBs and is kept after the run.

param.name: output_folder
param.displayname: Output Folder (used when container is 'One GDB per source KMZ')
param.dialogReference:
Folder that will receive one Geodatabase per source KMZ. Required when container (1) is "One GDB per source KMZ"; disabled otherwise.

Each output GDB is named after its source (e.g. ProjectA.gdb, HazardsReport.gdb). Scratch GDBs land in <folder>\_scratch\ inside the chosen folder and are kept after the run.

param.name: split_fds
param.dialogReference:
Whether each Feature Class lives inside a Feature Dataset (FD), or at the Geodatabase root.

CHECKED (default) -- FCs are organized inside FDs. Choose what becomes the FD name in 3a. The FC naming strategy in 3b shows options that work with FDs.

UNCHECKED -- No FDs. FCs sit at the GDB root. Question 3a is ignored; 3b shows root-level naming strategies instead.

Folder1, Folder2, Folder3 attribute columns are populated in either case (except when the FC naming strategy is "Leaf folder name", which encodes the leaf in the FC name itself).

param.name: fd_source
param.displayname: 3a. Feature Dataset source (only used if FDs are on)
param.dialogReference:
How the Feature Dataset name is derived. Only used when "Split output into Feature Datasets?" (2) is checked.

TOP FOLDER -- The third-from-deepest folder segment becomes the FD name (notation: segments[-3]). Example: a placemark in folder "Birds 2026 / BUOW / Known" yields FD "Birds_2026". Use when your KMZ is organized as "Category / Subcategory / Leaf" and you want one FD per Category.

TOP TWO FOLDERS COMBINED -- The two folders above the leaf are joined (notation: segments[-3] + segments[-2]). Example: "Birds 2026 / BUOW / Known" yields FD "Birds_2026_BUOW". Use for finer FD grouping when Category alone is too broad.

SOURCE KMZ FILENAME -- One FD per source KMZ file. Only available when container (1) is "One merged GDB" (in the per-KMZ container the GDB IS the source -- redundant). Best for traceability: the FD name tells you which KMZ a feature came from.

For folder paths shorter than 3 levels, the strategy falls back gracefully to whatever top segment is available.

param.name: fc_naming
param.dialogReference:
How each Feature Class is named. The available options change based on whether FDs are split (2).

PER-GEOMETRY ONLY -- "Points", "Polylines", "Polygons". Minimal naming; relies on the FD name (3a) and Folder1/2/3 attribute columns for context. Always available.

LEAF FOLDER NAME -- The leaf folder becomes the FC (e.g. "Known", "Suspected"). When two leaves in the same FD share a name across geometries, a geometry suffix is auto-added. Only available when 2 is checked AND 3a is "Top two folders combined".

LEAF + GEOMETRY -- FC name = <leaf>_<geom> (e.g. "Known_Points", "Suspected_Polygons"). Predictable: the geometry suffix is always visible. Good middle-ground when you want one FC per leaf but cleanly separated by geometry.

PARENT + LEAF + GEOMETRY -- FC name = <parent>_<leaf>_<geom> (e.g. "BUOW_Known_Points"). Most folder context preserved in the FC name.

FOLDER PAIR + GEOMETRY -- FC name = <top>_<parent>_<geom> (e.g. "Birds_2026_BUOW_Points"). The leaf moves into the Folder3 column. Best for compact symbology grouping when leaves are repetitive.

TOP FOLDER + GEOMETRY (only when 2 is unchecked) -- FC name = <top>_<geom> (e.g. "Birds_2026_Points"). All leaves under a top folder collapse into one FC, distinguished by Folder3 attribute.

If a resulting FC name exceeds the 64-character file-GDB limit, the leading folder portion is shortened automatically while the geometry suffix (e.g. "_Polygons") stays intact.

param.name: output_sr
param.dialogReference:
Target spatial reference for the output. Scratch GDBs are always WGS 1984; geometries project to this SR during Phase 2 and FDs/FCs are created in it.

SHORTLIST: WGS 1984 (pass-through); NAD 1983 HARN State Plane Washington N/S (US Ft); Oregon N/S (Intl Ft); California I-VI (US Ft); NAD 1983 UTM Zones 9N-12N (m).

TRANSFORMATION (auto-applied): HARN State Plane uses a 2-step chain WGS84 -- NAD83 -- HARN_state. UTM NAD83 uses a single step WGS84 -- NAD83. WGS 1984 applies no transformation.

KMZ source accuracy is typically ~5 m; pick the SR for your project deliverable, not the source data. Survey-grade work should not be sourced from KMZ.

param.name: follow_network_links
param.dialogReference: If on, the converter follows <NetworkLink> elements in source KMZs and downloads the referenced external KMZ/KML files inline. Useful for KMZs that are just wrappers around remote datasets (e.g. live database queries via Google Earth's NetworkLink mechanism). Off by default to avoid surprise network calls.

param.name: network_link_max_depth
param.dialogReference: How many levels of nested NetworkLinks to recurse. 1 = follow only the direct links in the source. 2 = also follow links found inside downloaded NetworkLink targets. Default 2. Ignored when "Follow NetworkLinks" is off.

param.name: network_link_timeout
param.dialogReference: Per-link HTTP timeout for NetworkLink downloads. Default 30 seconds. Increase on slow networks or when fetching large remote KMZs. Ignored when "Follow NetworkLinks" is off.
